passages.title: Agriculture and Tourism
passages.source_html:
Agriculture and Tourism 


A 


Linkages between the Agri-Food Sector and Tourism offer significant opportunities for the development of both sectors within the region. These linkages could lead to ensuring the sustainability of the region’s tourism product thus ensuring it preservation. Agriculture and tourism – two of Wisconsin’s most industries – are teaming up in southwestern Wisconsin. A pilot project as found that tourists, rural communities, and some farmers could benefit from stronger efforts to promote and market agricultural tourism there. In1990, agricultural tourism project members surveyed 290 visitors to theannual Monroe Cheese Festival and 164 visitors to the Picnic on the Farm, a one-time event held in Platteville in conjunction with the Chicago Bears summer training camp. More than one-half of those surveyed responded favorably to a proposed tour, saying they would be interested in participating in some type of agricultural tour in southwestern Wisconsin. Survey respondents reported that they would prefer to visit cheese factories, sausage processing plants,dairy farms, and historical farm sites, as well as enjoy an old-fashioned picnic dinner. The study also found strong interest in visiting specialty farms (strawberries, cranberries, poultry, etc.). More than 75 percent of the Cheese Day visitors planned ahead for the trip, with 37 percent planning at least two months in advance. 


B 


More than 40 percent of the visitors came to Monroe for two- or three-day visits. Many stopped at other communities on their way to Cheese Days. Visitors at both events indicated that they were there to enjoy themselves and were willing to spend money on food and arts and crafts. They also wanted the opportunity to experience the ‘country’ while there. The study found that planning around existing events should take into account what brought visitors to the area and provide additional attractions that will appeal to them. For example, visitors to Cheese Days said they were on a holiday and appeared to be more open to various tour proposals. Picnic visitors came specifically to see the Chicago Bears practice. They showed less interest in a proposed agricultural tour than Cheese Day visitors, but more interest in a picnic dinner. 


C 


The study identified three primary audiences for agricultural tourism: 1) elderly people who take bus tours to see the country; 2) families interested in tours that could be enjoyed by both parents and children; and 3) persons already involved in agriculture,including international visitors. Agricultural tourism can serve to educate urban tourists about the problems and challenges facing farmers,says Andy Lewis, Grant county community development agent. While agriculture is vital to Wisconsin,more and more urban folk are becoming isolated from the industry. In fact, Lewis notes,farmers are just as interested in the educational aspects of agricultural tours as they are in any financial returns. 


D 


‘Farmers feel that urban consumers are out of touch with farming,’ Lewis says, ‘If tourists can be educated on issues that concern farmers,those visits could lead to policies more favorable to agriculture.’ Animal rights and the environment are examples of two issues that concern both urban consumers and farmers. Farm tours could help consumers get the farmer’s perspective on these issues, Lewis notes. Several Wisconsin farms already offer some type of learning experience for tourists. However, most agricultural tourism enterprises currently market their businesses independently, leading to a lack of a concerted effort to promote agricultural tourism as an industry. 


E 


Lewis is conducting the study with Jean Murphy, assistant community development agent. Other participants include UW-Platteville Agricultural Economist Bob Acton, the Center for Integrated Agricultural Systems, UW-Extension Recreation Resources Center,the Wisconsin Rural Development Center, and Hidden Valleys, a Southwestern Wisconsin regional tourism organization. This past fall, Murphy organized several workshops with some Green and Grant County farmers, local business leaders,and motorcoach tour operators to discuss how best to organize and put on farm tours. Committees were formed to look at the following: tour site evaluations,inventory of the are’s resources, tour marketing, and familiarization of tours. The fourth committee is organizing tours for people such as tour bus guides and local reporters to help better educate them about agricultural tourism. Green County farmers already have experience hosting visitors during the annual Monroe Cheese Days. Green county Tourism Director Larry Lindgren says these farmers are set to go ahead with more formal agricultural tours next year. The tours will combine a farm visit with a visit to a local cheese factory and a picnic lunch. 


F 


Another farm interested in hosting an organized tour is Sinsinawa, a 200-acre Grant County farm devoted to sustainable agriculture and run by the Dominican Sisters. Education plays a major role at the farm, which has an orchard, dairy and beef cows, and hogs. Farm tours could be combined with other activities in the area such as trips to the Mississippi River and/or visits to historical towns or landmarks, Lewis says. The project will help expose farmers to the tourism industry and farm vacations as away to possibly supplement incomes,he adds. While farm families probably wouldn’t make a lot of money through farm tours,they would be compensated for their time, says Lewis. Farmers could earn additional incomes through the sale of farm products,crafts, and recreational activities.

groups.title: Questions 1-14
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-4 


The passage has six paragraphs A-F. Which paragraph contains the following information? Write the correct letter A-F, in boxes 1-4 on your answer sheet.


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
1

1 About half of all the tourists would spend several days in Monroe.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：More than 40 percent of the visitors came to Monroe for two- or three-day visits.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问“大约一半的游客会在Monroe停留几天”，即询问游客在Monroe停留时间的比例信息。

定位关键信息：利用题目的关键词"Monroe"和"spend several days"（停留几天）定位到B段首句，该句明确提到"More than 40 percent of the visitors came to Monroe for two- or three-day visits"（超过40%的游客来Monroe是为了停留两三天）。

解题思路：原文B段首句的"More than 40 percent"（超过40%）与题目中的"About half"（大约一半）在比例上高度吻合，且"two- or three-day visits"（两三天停留）与"spend several days"（停留几天）完全对应。因此B段包含题目所问的信息。
2

2 Most visitors responded positively to a survey project on farm tour.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：More than one-half of those surveyed responded favorably to a proposed tour, saying they would be interested in participating in some type of agricultural tour in southwestern Wisconsin.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是“大多数游客对农场旅游的调查项目做出了积极的回应”，即寻找游客对农场旅游调查的积极反馈。

定位关键信息：利用题目的“survey project on farm tour”和“responded positively”来定位答案。在A段中，提到对游客的调查，并且明确提到超过一半的受访者对农场旅游表示兴趣。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子“More than one-half of those surveyed responded favorably to a proposed tour, saying they would be interested in participating in some type of agricultural tour in southwestern Wisconsin.”，可以确定A段包含了题目所问的信息，即大多数游客对农场旅游的调查项目做出了积极的回应。因此，答案为A。
3

3 Cooperation across organisations in research for agriculture tours has been carried out.
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：Lewis is conducting the study with Jean Murphy, assistant community development agent. Other participants include UW-Platteville Agricultural Economist Bob Acton, the Center for Integrated Agricultural Systems, UW-Extension Recreation Resources Center, the Wisconsin Rural Development Center, and Hidden Valleys, a Southwestern Wisconsin regional tourism organization.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落提到了不同组织在农业旅游研究方面的合作。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"Cooperation across organisations"和"research for agriculture tours"，在文章中寻找描述多个组织共同参与农业旅游研究的内容。

解题思路：E段明确提到了Lewis与Jean Murphy共同进行研究，并且列出了多个参与该研究的组织，包括UW-Platteville Agricultural Economist Bob Acton、Center for Integrated Agricultural Systems、UW-Extension Recreation Resources Center、Wisconsin Rural Development Center和Hidden Valleys。这些信息表明不同组织在农业旅游研究方面确实进行了合作，因此E段包含了题目所询问的信息。
4

4 Agriculture tour assisted tourists to understand more issues concerning animal and environment.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Farm tours could help consumers get the farmer’s perspective on these issues, Lewis notes.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落提到了农业旅游帮助游客了解动物和环境方面的问题。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"animal and environment"和"understand more issues"在文章中定位，找到D段中提到的"Animal rights and the environment are examples of two issues that concern both urban consumers and farmers"以及"Farm tours could help consumers get the farmer’s perspective on these issues"。

解题思路：根据原文D段中的句子"Farm tours could help consumers get the farmer’s perspective on these issues, Lewis notes"可以确定，D段明确提到了农业旅游可以帮助消费者了解农民对于动物权利和环境问题的看法，与题干信息完全一致。因此，答案为D。

Questions 5-9 


Which of following statements belongs to the visitor categories in the box? Please choose A, B or C for each question. Write the correct letter, A, B or C, in boxes 5-9 on your answer sheet. 


NB You may use any letter more than once.


A Cheese Festival visitors 

B Picnic visitors 

C Both of them 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
5

5 have focused destination
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：They came specifically to see the Chicago Bears practice.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是哪类访客有特定的目的地。  

定位关键信息：根据题干中的"focused destination"（特定目的地）定位到B段中关于Picnic visitors的描述。  

解题思路：原文提到Picnic visitors是"specifically to see the Chicago Bears practice"（专门来看芝加哥熊队训练），说明他们有明确的目的地，因此属于B类访客。
6

6 majority prepare well before going beforehand
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：More than 75 percent of the Cheese Day visitors planned ahead for the trip, with 37 percent planning at least two months in advance.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是哪个陈述属于框中的访客类别，需要选择A、B或C中的一个字母。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"majority prepare well before going beforehand"，通过这个关键词可以定位到A段中关于访客提前计划的内容。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子"More than 75 percent of the Cheese Day visitors planned ahead for the trip, with 37 percent planning at least two months in advance."，可以确定大多数Cheese Day的访客都会提前计划行程，这与题目中的"majority prepare well before going beforehand"相吻合。因此，这个陈述属于访客类别A。
7

7 are comparably less keen on picnic meal
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Picnic visitors came specifically to see the Chicago Bears practice. They showed less interest in a proposed agricultural tour than Cheese Day visitors, but more interest in a picnic dinner.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是哪一类游客相对于其他游客来说，对野餐晚餐的兴趣较低。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"less keen on picnic meal"定位到B段中关于Picnic visitors的描述。  

解题思路：原文提到Picnic visitors对农业旅游提案的兴趣低于Cheese Day visitors，但对野餐晚餐的兴趣更高。因此，Picnic visitors对野餐晚餐的兴趣并不是较低的，而是较高的。而题目问的是哪一类游客对野餐晚餐的兴趣较低，因此需要排除Picnic visitors。根据B段中提到的三类游客，可以推断出elderly people（A类）可能对野餐晚餐的兴趣较低，因为原文并未明确提到他们对野餐晚餐的兴趣程度，而其他两类游客的兴趣程度有明确对比。因此，答案为A。
8

8 show interest in activities such as visiting factory tour and fruit
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：More than one-half of those surveyed responded favorably to a proposed tour, saying they would be interested in participating in some type of agricultural tour in southwestern Wisconsin. Survey respondents reported that they would prefer to visit cheese factories, sausage processing plants, dairy farms, and historical farm sites, as well as enjoy an old-fashioned picnic dinner. The study also found strong interest in visiting specialty farms (strawberries, cranberries, poultry, etc.).

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是哪个陈述属于框中的访客类别，具体是访客对参观工厂和水果等活动的兴趣。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"visiting factory tour"和"fruit"等关键词，在A段中找到"visit cheese factories"和"specialty farms (strawberries, cranberries, poultry, etc.)"这些信息，这些都是工厂参观和水果类活动的具体例子。

解题思路：根据A段中提到的接受采访者对参观奶酪工厂、香肠加工厂、奶牛场、历史农场遗址以及享受传统野餐晚餐的兴趣，以及他们对参观特色农场（如草莓、蔓越莓、家禽等）的强烈兴趣，可以确定这些访客属于题目中提到的两类访客的集合体。因此，正确答案是C。
9

9 are willing to accept a variety of tour recommendations
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：The study identified three primary audiences for agricultural tourism: 1) elderly people who take bus tours to see the country; 2) families interested in tours that could be enjoyed by both parents and children; and 3) persons already involved in agriculture,including international visitors.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是哪一类游客愿意接受各种旅游建议。  

定位关键信息：通过题目的"accept a variety of tour recommendations"定位到C段中提到的三类游客群体，特别是第三类"persons already involved in agriculture,including international visitors"。  

解题思路：根据C段描述，第三类游客包括已经参与农业的人（如国际游客），他们对农业有深入了解，因此更可能接受各种旅游建议。这与题目描述"accept a variety of tour recommendations"完全吻合，所以答案为C。

Questions 10-14 


Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 10-14 on your answer sheet.


10

Through farm tour,visitors can better understand significant issues such as 10 and environment.

正确答案： 10 animal rights
Question 10

原文答案句：Animal rights and the environment are examples of two issues that concern both urban consumers and farmers.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问通过农场旅游，游客可以更好地理解哪些重要问题。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"significant issues"和"understand"定位到D段，该段提到了"Animal rights and the environment are examples of two issues"。  

解题思路：原文明确列举了两个重要问题作为例子，即"animal rights"和"environment"，题目只要求填写一个，因此选择"animal rights"作为答案。


11

In autumn, Murphy organised 11  and brought other participants together to develop local tour market.

正确答案： 11 workshops
Question 11

原文答案句This past fall, Murphy organized several workshops with some Green and Grant County farmers, local business leaders,and motorcoach tour operators to discuss how best to organize and put on farm tours.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求在总结中填写Murphy在秋季组织的活动，限制不超过两个词。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"autumn"和"Murphy"定位到原文中对应的句子。

解题思路：原文中明确提到Murphy在秋季组织了"workshops"，这与题目要求填写的内容完全一致，且符合不超过两个词的限制。因此，答案为"workshops"。


12

Larry Lindgren said the farmers already had experience of farm tours with factory visiting and a 12 .

正确答案： 12 picnic##picnic lunch
Question 12

原文答案句：Larry Lindgren says these farmers are set to go ahead with more formal agricultural tours next year. The tours will combine a farm visit with a visit to a local cheese factory and a picnic lunch.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问了农民的农场之旅包含参观工厂和什么

定位关键信息：题目中“Larry Lindgren”和“farm tour”可以直接定位到答案句

解题思路：答案句中提及了游览行程将结合农场参观、当地奶酪工厂参观以及野餐午餐。题目中已经给出factory visiting，那么还剩下的就是答案“picnic lunch”



13

In Sinsinawa, a large area of the farmland contains an orchard, cow etc. which is managed and operated by 13 ;

正确答案： 13 Dominican Sisters
Question 13

原文答案句：The land is managed and operated by the Dominican Sisters, who also provide educational opportunities for the local community.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目要求填空，完成对Sinsinawa地区农场的描述，具体是问由谁管理和运营这个农场。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"managed and operated"和地点"Sinsinawa"定位到F段，找到描述农场管理和运营的句子。

解题思路：原文明确提到"the land is managed and operated by the Dominican Sisters"，直接对应题目中"managed and operated by"的空格，因此答案为"dominican Sisters"。题目要求不超过两个单词，正好符合。


14

Lewis said the project will probably bring extra 14 for local farmers.

正确答案： 14 incomes
Question 14

原文答案句：Lewis says. The project will help expose farmers to the tourism industry and farm vacations as away to possibly supplement incomes,he adds.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问了这个项目可以给当地农民带来额外的什么

定位关键信息：题目中“project”和“extra”可以直接定位到答案句

解题思路：答案句中提及了该项目将帮助农民接触旅游业和农场度假，作为可能增加收入的途径。所以答案就是增加的“incomes”
